name: omarchy_updates
content:
Updates 
Omarchy and your packages are kept up to date via Update > Omarchy in the Omarchy
menu (Super + Alt + Space).
This pus the atest Omarchy code and configs, runs any pending migrations to get your
system in sync with the atest, and updates a system packages from the Omarchy Arch
Mirror, Omarchy Package Repository, and AUR (if you have instaed any AUR packages).
When new reeases are made, a circe arrow icon wi appear to the right of your cock.
Cick it and the update process wi start.
Three channes 
Omarchy is updated aong three channes: stabe, edge, and dev. New instaations start
on the stabe channe, which tracks the officia reeases, as we as the stabe Omarchy
Arch mirror that's running one month behind the atest, so we can catch any new
incompatibiities that require config changes before they cause probems for peope.
But if you'd ike to hep spot those potentia issues, you can run on the edge channe.
That' keep your Omarchy code tracking officia reeases, but ets you update to the atest
Arch packages as soon as they're avaiabe. You shoud ony do this if you're experienced
with Linux, and know how to recover a system that has probems.
Finay, there's the dev channe, which gives you the very atest Omarchy code changes
and the edge packages. You shoud ony use this channe if you're an experienced Linux
user, working directy on Omarchy, and wiing to toerate breakage.
You can switch between these channes using Update > Channe from the Omarchy
menu.
Warning about direct pacman/yay updates 
Updates · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/68/updates
1 of 2 2/2/26, 1:26 AM

---

Warning about direct pacman/yay updates 
If you're aready famiiar with Arch, you might be tempted to just run pacman -Syu  or yay
-Syu yoursef, but if you do that, you run the risk that you' miss updates to the
configuration fies needed to support newer versions of ibraries or toos. So it's best to
stick with Update > Omarchy, so you're sure that any migrations are run together with new
packages.
Roing back bad updates 
If you ever have a probem after doing an update, you can roback your system to the
snapshot taken before the update. Just restart and pick the snapshot in the boot oading
menu from before you started the update.
If somehow your configuration fies have been corrupted, you can aso perform an
Omarchy reinsta using omarchy-reinstall in the termina. This wi restore your Omarchy
instaation to the atest reease, put you on stabe and downgrade any packages, and
reset a the configuration fies. Note that a your user config changes to the Omarchy
defauts wi be overwritten doing this!
Updates · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/68/updates
